text: Agile methodology is a popular approach in software development. It focuses on iterative development, meaning the project is built in small, repeatable cycles. Each cycle is called a sprint, usually lasting two to four weeks. During a sprint, the team works on a set of tasks from a prioritized list known as the backlog. These tasks are described as user stories, which explain what a user needs from the software. Every day, the team holds a short meeting called a standup, where members share their progress and any problems. By working in this flexible, step-by-step way, Agile teams can quickly adapt to changes and deliver value to users early and often.
